stories.h: Apple opens its on-device foundation models to every iOS app
stories.s: Free, private, offline AI in a billion pockets — a quiet threat to per-token API businesses.
b: New framework gives third-party apps access to the on-device 7B model.
No API costs and no data leaves the phone.
Developers say quality now rivals last year's cloud models.

stories.h: Report: OpenAI in talks to raise at a $500B valuation
stories.s: Half a trillion dollars for a nine-year-old lab — the market is pricing in AGI-scale outcomes.
b: SoftBank and existing investors reportedly leading.
Funds would go to compute buildout under the Stargate program.
Revenue run-rate reportedly approaching $20B.

stories.h: Runway's Gen-5 video model demo floods social feeds
stories.s: Minute-long coherent scenes with dialogue — film-industry disruption talk is back, louder.
b: Generates 60-second clips with consistent characters and lip-synced dialogue.
Hollywood unions immediately called for contract protections.
Public access starts next month at $95/mo.

stories.h: UK gives every university researcher free frontier-model compute
stories.s: A national bet that academic AI research shouldn't depend on big-lab goodwill.
b: £800M program covers compute credits across all UK universities.
Includes access to a sovereign 100k-GPU cluster.
Modeled on CERN-style shared infrastructure.

stories.h: Paper: LLMs show human-like memory interference patterns
stories.s: Models forget the way we do — a clue that they may organize knowledge more like brains than databases.
b: New memories overwrite similar old ones, mirroring human interference effects.
Held across five model families tested.
Could inform better fine-tuning that avoids catastrophic forgetting.

stories.h: Waymo goes fully driverless in Miami, its 9th city
stories.s: Robotaxis are quietly becoming boring — which is exactly what winning looks like.
b: No safety driver from day one for the first time.
Now running 500k+ paid rides weekly across all cities.
Miami adds rain and flood-zone driving to the public test set.

stories.h: Hugging Face passes 5 million hosted models
stories.s: The long tail of AI is enormous — most of these are tiny fine-tunes, and that's the point.
b: Up from 1M just two years ago.
Fastest-growing category: small on-device specialist models.

stories.h: Researchers warn benchmark saturation is breaking model comparison
stories.s: When every model scores 95%+, the tests stop telling you anything — expect a new generation of evals.
b: 12 of 20 standard benchmarks now show ceiling effects.
Private, rotating eval sets gaining traction as the fix.
